Grad Cafe Analysis App
  • API Reference
  • View page source

API Reference

This section documents the main Python modules in the application using Sphinx autodoc.

Flask Application

app.build_filter_where_clause(filters)[source]

Builds a dynamic SQL WHERE clause based on dashboard filters. Uses parameters to avoid unsafe string formatting.

app.create_app(test_config=None)[source]
app.fetch_all(cursor, query, params=None)[source]
app.fetch_one(cursor, query, params=None)[source]
app.get_analysis_results()[source]
app.get_connection()[source]

Creates a PostgreSQL database connection.

If DATABASE_URL is available, it is used first. This is useful for tests and GitHub Actions. Otherwise, the app falls back to the local PostgreSQL settings used for development.

app.get_dashboard_results(filters)[source]

Gets interactive dashboard metrics based on selected filters.

app.get_filter_options()[source]

Gets dropdown filter options from the database.

app.run_pull_data_pipeline()[source]

Runs the incremental Pull Data pipeline in the background.

Database Utilities

Database utility functions for testing and inserting Grad Café applicant records.

db_utils.clear_applicants_table(connection)[source]

Removes all rows from the applicants table.

Parameters:

connection – Active psycopg database connection.

Returns:

None.

db_utils.count_applicants(connection)[source]

Counts rows in the applicants table.

Parameters:

connection – Active psycopg database connection.

Returns:

Number of applicant rows.

db_utils.create_applicants_table(connection)[source]

Creates the applicants table using the required Module 3 schema.

Parameters:

connection – Active psycopg database connection.

Returns:

None.

db_utils.get_analysis_summary(connection)[source]

Returns a small analysis summary dictionary used for tests.

Parameters:

connection – Active psycopg database connection.

Returns:

Dictionary with expected analysis keys.

db_utils.get_schema_columns(connection)[source]

Returns column names for the applicants table.

Parameters:

connection – Active psycopg database connection.

Returns:

List of column names.

db_utils.insert_applicants(connection, records)[source]

Inserts applicant records into the applicants table.

Duplicate p_id values are ignored so repeated pulls do not create duplicates.

Parameters:

  • connection – Active psycopg database connection.

  • records – List of applicant dictionaries.

Returns:

Number of rows actually inserted.

Data Loading

load_data.clean_date(value)[source]

Converts date strings into YYYY-MM-DD format. .. rubric:: Example

“Added on May 29, 2026” -> “2026-05-29”

load_data.clean_float(value)[source]

Converts messy numeric strings into floats. .. rubric:: Examples

“GPA 3.90” -> 3.90 “165” -> 165.0 None -> None

load_data.clean_status(value)[source]

Converts status strings into a simple admission decision. .. rubric:: Examples

“Accepted on May 29” -> “Accepted” “Rejected on May 29” -> “Rejected” “Wait listed” -> “Wait listed”

load_data.create_table(cursor)[source]

Drops and recreates the applicants table.

load_data.insert_records(cursor, records)[source]

Inserts applicant records into PostgreSQL.

load_data.load_json_data()[source]

Loads the JSON applicant records from the data folder.

load_data.main()[source]

Query Helpers

query_data.fetch_all(cursor, query, params=None)[source]
query_data.fetch_one(cursor, query, params=None)[source]
query_data.get_connection()[source]
query_data.main()[source]
